original: Ronaldo, a 6-foot Brazilian rainbow boa constrictor, gives birth to 14 babies in Portsmouth, England, despite no contact with another snake, by parthenogenesis (asexual reproduction)
"Harry Potter and the Order of the Phoenix" the 5th book of the series byJ. K. Rowlingis published worldwide in English
SpaceShipOne becomes the first privately funded spaceplane to achieve spaceflight
First Ferris wheel opens at the Columbian Exposition in Chicago, Illinois
US approves chicken made from animal cells - the country's first lab-grown meat, aimed at reducing harm to animals and the environment
Disney animated movie "Lilo & Stitch" released, written and directed by Chris Sanders and Dean DeBlois
English mathematician Andrew Wiles proves the last theorem of French mathematicianPierre de Fermatafter 356 years, solving the world's most difficult math problem
New archaeological discovery announced near Stonehenge of a large circle of shafts surrounding a village 2500 BC, largest prehistoric structure in Britain
Pluto's newly discovered moons are officially named Nix and Hydra
1st stored computer program runs on Manchester Mark I at a laboratory in Manchester University, England
Qhapaq Ñan, the Andean Road System of the Inca Empire, 30,000km long (18,600 miles) through six countries, granted World Heritage status by UNESCO
FIFA World Cup Final, Estadio Azteca, Mexico City: Brazil and Pelé become the first team and player to win the World Cup three times, defeating Italy 4-1 in front of 107,412 spectators
At Yankee Stadium rally, Nelson Mandela dons a NY Yankee baseball cap and proclaims "I am a Yankee!"
Mexican artistFrida Kahlois the 1st Hispanic woman to be honored on a US postage stamp
NY Yankees announceLou Gehrig's retirement after doctors reveal he has amyotrophic lateral sclerosis
Carlton Fisk becomes AL catcher HR hitter, 307th at Yankee stadium
Total solar eclipse in Madagascar and the Indian Ocean lasts for 4 minutes 56 seconds
South Korea successfully launches its first satellites into orbit with its homegrown rocket Nuri, from Naro Space Center
Daniel Carter Beard, American author, social reformer and founder of the Boy Scouts of America, born in Cincinnati, Ohio (d. 1941)
Heike Drechsler of East Germany long jumps 7.45 m (women's record)
1985 Heisman Trophy winnerBo Jackson, signs 3-year contract to play baseball with the Kansas City Royals
USAF Major Robert M. White takes X-15 to 75,190 m
129°F (54°C), Tirat Zevi, Israel (Asian record)
ICC Men's Cricket ODI World Cup, Lord's: West Indies beat Australia by 17 runs to win inaugural final; Player of the match:Clive Lloyd(WI) 102 (85)
ICC Women's Cricket T20 World Cup, Lord's: England 86/4 win inaugural event; beat New Zealand 85; England's Katherine Brunt takes 3 for 6
ICC Men's Cricket T20 World Cup, Lord's, London: Pakistan wins their first title beating Sri Lanka by 8 wickets; Player of the Series: Sri Lankan all-rounder Tillakaratne Dilshan (317 runs)
German sprinter Armin Hary runs world record 100m (10.0)
US Senator Daniel K. Inouye and 19 other Japanese-American WWII veterans of the 442nd Regiment belatedly awarded the Medal of Honor by PresidentBill Clinton
The Netherlands returns 119 sculptures from the Benin Bronzes collection that were looted from the Kingdom of Benin (present-day Nigeria) by colonial forces

Columbia Records unveils the 33-1/3 rpm LP phonograph record, invented byPeter Carl Goldmark, allowing up to 20 minutes per side (available in 10 and 12-inch diameters) at the Waldorf-Astoria Hotel, NYC; over the next decade, its popularity and profitability push the 78 rpm record out of production
Louis Ottens, Dutch engineer, developed the audio cassette tape, and worked on the compact disc, born in Bellingwolde, Netherlands (d. 2021)[1]
American inventor and businessmanCyrus McCormickpatents the reaping machine
US Constitution comes into effect when New Hampshire is the 9th state to ratify it
Frank Winfield Woolworthopens his first successful "Woolworth's Great Five Cent Store" on North Queen Street, Lancaster, Pennsylvania
German tennis superstarSteffi Grafbecomes first defending champion to lose in the 1st round of a major tournament (Wimbledon to American Lorrie McNeal)
Women's National Basketball Association begins as NY Liberty beats LA Sparks
Hackers ground 1400 passengers by attacking IT system at Warsaw Chopin airport in Poland
Carl Nassib becomes the first openly gay player in the NFL in a post on Instagram
MLB Los Angeles Angels designated hitterShohei Ohtanihas career high 8 RBI in 11-inning 12-11 loss to Kansas City Royals in Anaheim, California
NHL Draft: Sault Ste. Marie Greyhounds (OHL) centerJoe Thorntonfirst pick by Boston Bruins
NHL Draft: US NTDP center Jack Hughes first pick by New Jersey Devils
NBA Draft: Arizona center Deandre Ayton first pick by Phoenix Suns
Lionel Rose, Australian boxer (WBA, WBC, The Ring bantamweight title 1968-69; first Indigenous Australian to win a world title), born in Drouin, Australia (d. 2011)
Edward Anders [Edward Alperovitch], Latvian-American cosmochemist known for his work on how wildfires contributed to dinosaurs' extinction, born in Liepāja, Latvia (d. 2025)[1]
Clara Immerwahr, German chemist, first woman to be awarded a doctorate in chemistry in Germany, born in Polkendorf, Silesia Province, Kingdom of Prussia (d. 1915)
Charles Momsen, American inventor (submarine rescue device), born in Flushing, New York (d. 1967)
Joseph Rainey, American politician, 1st African American in US House of Representatives (1870-1879), born in Georgetown, South Carolina (d. 1887)
New York IslandersDenis PotvinandMike Bossyare elected to the Hockey Hall of Fame
The first successful west-to-east navigation of Northwest Passage begins at Vancouver, British Columbia
HMT Empire Windrush with the first 800 emigrants from the West Indies to the UK arrives at Port of Tilbury near London
Ellen Louks Fairclough is sworn in as Canada's first woman Cabinet Minister
Discovery rolls over from OPF to Vehicle Assembly Building at Kennedy Space Center (Florida)
STS-57 (Endeavour) launches into orbit
Severe flooding affects southern China, including Guangdong and Jiangxi provinces, with areas experiencing their highest rainfall since 1961, forcing authorities to evacuate tens of thousands
Saudi Arabia bans international visitors from making the Islamic Hajj pilgrimage in 2020 due to COVID-19
Louis Braille's remains are transferred to the Panthéon in Paris, while his hands are kept as relics at Coupvray cemetery
Louisiana chess prodigyPaul Morphyarrives in Europe
Johnny Cashdebuts Top 10 country song "Cry! Cry! Cry!"
Zager & Evans release "In the Year 2525"
50,000 attend Celebration of Life, rock concert in McCrea, Louisiana; performers includedChuck Berry; Stephen Stills; WAR; John Sebastian; and Delaney and Bonnie
